title: contingency-dsl Composed — Autoshaping / Sign-Tracking
description: Composed procedure schema for autoshaping (Brown & Jenkins, 1968). A localized CS (key light, inserted lever) is paired with a US via forward Pavlovian contingency; no operant response is programmed, yet subjects emit directed CS-engagement responses (sign-tracking) and/or US-location-approach responses (goal-tracking). Classified as composed rather than operant because the diagnostic omission procedure (schema/composed/omission.schema.json) shows the response is under Pavlovian control.

AutoshapeProcedure.description: Pavlovian forward-delay pairing with a localized CS. The emergent response topography (sign-tracking vs goal-tracking) is an observed outcome, not a grammar-level distinction.

cs.description: Localized CS (e.g., 'key_light', 'inserted_lever'). The linter SHOULD verify via @cs annotation modality that the CS is localized in the chamber; non-localized CSs (e.g., diffuse tone) do not reliably produce autoshaping.

us.description: US delivered at CS offset. Typically appetitive (food, sucrose). US delivery is response-independent.

pairing.description: Respondent primitive. Standard autoshaping uses Pair.ForwardDelay; trace variants are admitted to preserve the composed structure across procedural variations.

topography.description: Advisory field documenting the expected or observed response topography. 'observed' (default) indicates the program does not commit to a topography ex ante; measurement annotations record what actually occurred.
